title: Amazon Stock Trends in Canada
description: Investors in Canada are discussing whether to buy Amazon stock despite its underperformance compared to S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 since Jeff Bezos stepped down as CEO, according to The Globe and Mail.

sources.title: Should You Buy Amazon Stock, Even Though It Has Badly Underperformed the S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 Since Jeff Bezos Stepped Down as CEO?
Truist reiterates Buy on Amazon stock, keeps $320 target on AWS growth
Amazon’s AI Arms Race: KeyBanc Says Massive Capex Is a Feature, Not a Bug

sections.heading: Amazon Stock Performance
paragraphs: Amazon stock has badly underperformed the S&P 500 and Nasdaq-100 since Jeff Bezos stepped down as CEO, as reported by The Globe and Mail.

sections.heading: Analyst Recommendations
paragraphs: Investing.com Canada reports that Truist reiterates a Buy rating on Amazon stock and maintains a $320 target due to AWS growth.

sections.heading: Amazon's AI and Capital Expenditure
paragraphs: Benzinga discusses Amazon's AI arms race and KeyBanc's view that massive capital expenditure is a feature, not a bug.

faq.a: According to The Globe and Mail, Amazon stock has underperformed since Jeff Bezos stepped down as CEO.

faq.q: What is the analyst's view on Amazon stock?
faq.a: Investing.com Canada states that Truist maintains a Buy rating on Amazon stock with a $320 target price due to AWS growth.

faq.q: What is Amazon's stance on AI and CapEx?
faq.a: Benzinga reports that KeyBanc views Amazon's massive capital expenditure as a feature, highlighting their commitment to AI.
